caption:
Google just changed how Local Services Ads work.

Starting August 2026, LSAs move inside Google Ads as Performance Max pay-per-lead campaigns for a first group of US advertisers, with the rest following through 2027.

The catch: Target CPA becomes one number per campaign. Run plumbing and HVAC leads together and Google sets one blended cost-per-lead target for both, unless you split them into separate campaigns first.

Also changing: your business name, address, and hours now sync straight from your Google Business Profile into the new campaign setup. A significant edit can trigger a 24-48 hour verification review that pauses your ads.

This week: pull your last 90 days of LSA data by service category. Cost per lead, close rate, average job value.

That tells you whether one campaign still works for you or you need to split before the migration reaches your account.

Want the Black Arrow team to audit your account and manage the transition?

DM SCALE

#LocalServicesAds #GoogleAds #HomeServicesMarketing #ContractorMarketing #LeadGeneration
rationale: Fills the under-served News/Industry Intelligence slot with a genuinely new, dated Google Ads platform change specific to home-services advertisers, delivered as a reel since research shows Reels still lead reach for cold-audience news content.
